title: The Result of the TIMEDIFF Function Has Issues
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```sql
SELECT TIMEDIFF('2020-11-11 01:01:01', '2020-11-11 01:02:01') 
```

<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

MariaDB says:

```sql
-00:01:00
```

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

TiDB says:

```sql
00:01:00
```

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->

Tested in two versions:

```
Release Version: v6.6.0-serverless
Edition: Community
Git Commit Hash: 2969dfb0e28d6c700405be432132f9e753a79b16
Git Branch: release-6.6-serverless
UTC Build Time: 2023-03-21 05:46:43
GoVersion: go1.19.4
Race Enabled: false
TiKV Min Version: 6.1.0
Check Table Before Drop: false
Store: tikv
```

```
Release Version: v6.1.1
Edition: Community
Git Commit Hash: 5263a0abda61f102122735049fd0dfadc7b7f8b2
Git Branch: heads/refs/tags/v6.1.1
UTC Build Time: 2022-08-25 10:43:14
GoVersion: go1.18.5
Race Enabled: false
TiKV Min Version: v3.0.0-60965b006877ca7234adaced7890d7b029ed1306
Check Table Before Drop: false
```
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE time_test (id INT PRIMARY KEY, start_time TIMESTAMP, end_time TIMESTAMP, duration INTERVAL);
INSERT INTO time_test (id, start_time, end_time) VALUES (1, '2020-11-11 01:01:01', '2020-11-11 01:02:01');
INSERT INTO time_test (id, start_time, end_time) VALUES (2, '2020-11-11 01:00:00', '2020-11-11 01:01:00');
SELECT id, (end_time - start_time) AS time_difference FROM time_test WHERE id = 1;
key_bug_features: TIMEDIFF function returns incorrect time difference format
